broadcast.text: Five picks, five starters. Now we're shopping for the bench and everybody else is panic-buying quarterbacks. Love our seat.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Three quarterbacks went in six picks and the room still left Herbert, Prescott, and Lawrence on the table. Patience pays at the position everyone panics about.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Loveland was the grown-up pick and now we plug the backfield. Swift's boring. Boring wins Tuesdays.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Middle rounds now and the quarterbacks are dangling — Burrow at fifty-three is the kind of thing that decides leagues in December.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Three wideouts in four rounds and every one of them was the best player sitting there. You take the gifts, you say thank you.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Quarterbacks finally moved — Maye and Jackson back to back — and the room let DeVonta Smith fall out of the thirties.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: You don't pass a top-17 talent at pick 29 over a bye week, people. That's coupon-clipping at the caviar counter.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: A.J. Brown sliding to the back of round three is the first real board break of the night. Somebody's getting a discount.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Puka plus Chase Brown through two rounds — that's a foundation, folks. Byes 11 and 6, no stacking problem, no panic.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Nine running backs went in the first nineteen picks. If you wanted one, the shelf is down to Chase Brown and hope.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Puka to the good guys, book it. Best player available AND he doesn't share a bye with anybody we care about. Draft room's cooking.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Four picks in and the running back run is already on — Robinson, McCaffrey, Gibbs gone. The board says the value pick here is a wideout.
broadcast.speaker: anchor
